title: The Door Guard — Night Approach
opening:
The alley is dark. {{NPC_NAME}} leans against the reinforced door, one hand resting on a weapon. They clock you the moment you round the corner.

{{NPC_NAME}}: Don't move. State your business.

npc_intent.baseline_belief: Strangers arriving at night are threats until proven otherwise. The Insurgency's survival depends on my judgment here.
npc_intent.long_term_desire: Protect the Insurgency and prove my worth as its first line of defence
npc_intent.immediate_intention: Assess and either admit or turn away this stranger
npc_intent.stakes: Insurgency security, operational secrecy, and the guard's own standing

nodes._comment: First contact. NPC is on alert — one turn only. Always routes out after turn 1.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You just clocked this stranger coming out of the dark. Size them up in one sharp line — make them feel the weight of your suspicion. You haven't decided anything yet.

transitions._comment: Hostile or careless opener — immediate rejection
Rare: genuine connection signal on first move with already-warm relation
Standard first-turn progression — fires after turn 1 if no earlier match

choices.text: I've seen what's happened to this land. People are suffering. I want to help make things right.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Easy words. Everyone claims they care when it suits them.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: At least you see the suffering. That's more than most.

choices.text: I don't need to prove anything to you. I've fought for survival just like you have. Open the door.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: You think you can intimidate ME? Wrong move.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Bold. I respect that. But respect isn't a pass.

choices.text: Look, we both know the Insurgency needs allies. I have skills you need. Let's not waste time with tests.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Everyone thinks they're clever. What makes you different?
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Skills, huh? Maybe. But I've seen too many smooth talkers.

choices.text: I understand your caution. Let me explain why our goals align and how we can help each other.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Mutual benefit? Everyone says that until it costs them something.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: You're thinking strategically. I appreciate that. Keep talking.

nodes._comment: Main mid-game test loop. NPC presses for cracks. Routes to probing_deep, negotiate, succeed, or fail. Most players spend 2-3 turns here.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You're not satisfied yet. Push back directly on what they just said. Ask something specific — force them to demonstrate real commitment, not just words. This is a test.

transitions._comment: NPC fully convinced — accepting intention + high relation
Transactional deal offered — NPC cautiously willing
NPC still evaluating — escalate depth of test

choices.text: I've already lost everything. My family, my home. I have nothing left but the will to fight.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Loss doesn't make you special. We've all lost.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Then you understand. That loss — that rage — use it well.

choices.text: I can get supplies from the outside. Food, medicine, weapons. Things you need.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Supplies? We'll see. Actions speak louder.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Now you're speaking my language. Supplies are always useful.

choices.text: Let me work the outer perimeter first. Prove myself where the stakes are lower.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Smart. At least you're thinking ahead.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: That's actually reasonable. I respect the tactical approach.

choices.text: I don't need to be special. I just need to be useful. And I am.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Useful? Prove it. Until then, you're just noise.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Confidence. I like it. But confidence without proof means nothing.

choices.text: The Insurgency needs to think beyond survival. We need to build a future worth fighting for.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Philosophy won't stop bullets. We need action, not words.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: You're not wrong. But survival comes first. Then we talk about the future.

choices.text: That's a heavy question. Can we discuss this inside?

interaction_outcomes.min_response: I thought so. You're just another coward.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Avoiding the question tells me everything I need to know.

nodes._comment: NPC heard the player out but is still not convinced. They push harder now — looking for contradictions, demanding something concrete. DOES NOT loop back to probing. Exits to succeed, negotiate, or fail only. Turn-count safety net at turn 6 forces negotiate to prevent soft-lock.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You've heard them. You're still not sure. Push harder — find the contradiction or demand something concrete and irreversible. Give them one real chance to seal it. If they dodge again, it's over.

transitions._comment: Player earned full trust: accepting intention + sufficient relation
Authority path: bold challenge earned mutual respect
Player is engaging but needs conditional terms
ANTI-SOFTLOCK: after 6 turns force progression to negotiate regardless of intention match

choices.text: I'm not here to waste your time. Put me where I'm most useful and I'll prove it.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: You sound sure of yourself. We'll see if that holds.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: You keep saying the right things. Maybe you're worth the risk.

choices.text: You keep testing me. When do I get to test you?

interaction_outcomes.min_response: You've got guts. I'll give you that.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Fair point. You've earned some trust. Don't waste it.

choices.text: You and I both know the old world is gone. What we build now decides everything. That's worth fighting for together.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Pretty words. But words don't win wars.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: You actually believe that. That's... rare. Come in.

nodes._comment: NPC offers conditional entry. Player can accept cleanly, push back on terms, or blow it at the last moment. Exits to succeed or fail ONLY — no loops back into probing.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You've decided to offer conditional entry. State your terms plainly. Make it clear what you expect and what they get in return. Don't be warm — this is still a transaction, not a welcome.

transitions._comment: Relation floor — if it dropped this low during negotiate, cut it
Relation ceiling — high trust overrides everything, just let them through
Clean acceptance — player took the terms
Player pushed back on terms — NPC respected the boldness
Player blew it at the last step

choices.text: Fair enough. What do you need me to do?

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Good. We'll see if you can back it up.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Reasonable. I'll brief you on what's needed.

choices.text: I'll do it, but I want something in return. Information. Access.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Careful. Push too hard and the deal's off.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: You drive a hard bargain. We'll see what can be arranged.

nodes._comment: TERMINAL — player earned entry. NPC gives reluctant but genuine access.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You've decided to trust them — reluctantly, but genuinely. Welcome them in a way that makes clear this is earned, not freely given. One short, final line.
nodes.terminal_result: Guard grants entry to the Insurgency hideout. Conversation ends.

nodes._comment: TERMINAL — player failed to convince or actively burned the NPC. Door stays shut.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You've decided they're not trustworthy, or they pushed too hard and burned whatever goodwill existed. Close the door. Final. No negotiation.
nodes.terminal_result: Guard refuses entry. Player is turned away. Conversation ends.
